title: Fixup how commands show up as part of the Runner API
body: When injecting commands to the runner, the stubs generated were not accurate or complete. This addresses this issue

step_plan.summary: Fix Runner API stub generation so dynamically injected CLI subcommands appear completely and with correct names, docstrings, and signatures. This is achieved by preserving/attaching callable signature metadata at injection time and teaching the stub generator to use that metadata when emitting Runner class stubs.

steps.operation: Preserve and expose accurate CLI command metadata/signatures for dynamically injected Runner methods
steps.reason: Runner subcommands are injected as wrapper methods with a generic `(*args, **kwargs)` signature, which causes runtime-introspection stub generation to emit incomplete/incorrect stubs. Attach the underlying callable and/or its `inspect.Signature` to the injected method (e.g., via `__signature__`, `__wrapped__`, and/or a class-level mapping like `_runner_injected_methods[name] = runner_subcommand`) so stubgen can recover the real API surface reliably.
steps.side_effects: May change how `help()`, IDEs, and introspection report Runner methods (now showing the underlying signature). If `__signature__` is set, downstream code relying on the old generic signature could observe differences. Ensure wrapper still forwards args correctly and remains compatible with all injected commands.

steps.operation: Extend class stub generation to special-case Runner and emit stubs for injected commands using preserved metadata
steps.reason: The stub generator already needs explicit handling for metaclass/dynamic injection patterns (as noted for other classes). Add Runner-specific logic to (a) discover injected Runner methods (preferably via a mapping/metadata installed by `RunnerMeta.__new__`), (b) include all injected commands in the class stub (ensuring completeness), and (c) use the underlying callable/signature/docstring rather than the wrapper’s `*args, **kwargs`.
steps.side_effects: Generated `metaflow-stubs` will change (more methods and/or corrected signatures). Any tests that snapshot stubs will need updates. If discovery relies on a new attribute (e.g., `_runner_injected_methods`), stubgen becomes coupled to that contract and should handle older versions gracefully (fallback to current behavior if missing).

steps.operation: Allow function stub generation to accept an override signature/callable for injected Runner methods and render it correctly
steps.reason: To avoid duplicating signature formatting logic, adjust `_generate_function_stub` to support a provided signature (or an alternate callable such as the underlying runner CLI function) when generating a method stub. This ensures Runner-injected methods can be emitted with accurate positional/keyword-only args, defaults, and annotations even when the runtime wrapper differs.
steps.side_effects: May affect stub output for other dynamically generated methods if they start using this override mechanism. Requires careful handling of bound-method `self` (drop/keep first parameter as appropriate) and consistent rendering of annotations/defaults to avoid breaking existing stub formatting expectations.
